---
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.book-home}

<img src="assets/images/data-r-155-banner.png" class="home-banner-strip" alt="DATA-R 155 Introduction to R Programming course banner.">

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

::: {.home-intro-card}
## Guided activities for learning R workflow

This book contains the guided activities for **Introduction to R Programming**. These activities help you move between reading instructions, typing code in RStudio, checking what happened, and reflecting on what you are learning.

You are not expected to know this before you begin. The goal is not perfect code on the first try. The goal is to build a reliable workflow for noticing, checking, and improving your work.
:::

## What this book is

This book is a working collection of guided R activities. Each chapter introduces course concepts, models R workflow, gives you code to type in RStudio, and prompts you to document what you are learning.

The guided activities are instructional support. They help you learn the material before, during, and after class.

## What this book is not

This book is not the same thing as the **Practice Space** assignment you submit for evaluation.

The guided activity helps you learn. The Practice Space is where you show evidence of what you learned. Some chapters include a Practice Space section so you can see how the guided work connects to the submitted work, but you should always follow the Canvas assignment directions for what to submit.

## How to use this book

Keep this book open next to RStudio.

Use each chapter to:

- read the overview,
- type the code yourself,
- check what happened,
- write down what worked,
- notice what confused you,
- and use the summary and chapter terms when you review.

## When something does not work

Confusion is not a sign that you are failing. It is information you can use to decide what to check next.

When code does not work, slow down and ask:

- What was my goal?
- What did I type?
- What happened?
- What did I expect to happen?
- What can I check next?

## Start here

Begin with the **Preface**, then move into the first guided activity in **Tenacity**.

<div class="home-button-row">
  <a class="home-button-primary" href="front-matter/preface.html">Start with the Preface</a>
  <a class="home-button-secondary" href="chapters/01-tenacity/ch01-navigation.html">Open Chapter 1</a>
</div>

:::
